goal: Fixes weren't visible (cached CSS); Celtic result match poster format + uniform sizes; poster label overlap
root_cause: CSS was referenced with NO version query (css/styles.css …) so browsers cached it indefinitely — every CSS fix looked 'not done' (origin verified serving latest). Celtic on-screen used an empty-left-col 5-col that looked right-shifted with mismatched centre/crossing sizes. Poster drew per-card name+orientation (~64px) below each card → overlapped the next stacked card (row gap ~20px).
fix: (1) Version-stamped css/js/share refs (?v=20260619a) across the 8 app pages + /th/ so browsers refetch on deploy. (2) css/styles.css celtic @640: poster format — cross cols1-3, gap col4, staff col5, block width:max-content + margin auto (centred), all cards uniform 60px. (3) poster.js: per-card label reduced to the position pill only (dropped name+orientation) + pill shrunk/tightened so it fits the row gap (no spill onto the next card).
verified: chrome MCP @390 celtic result: uniform 60px cards, cross+gap+staff centred block. Live origin confirmed serving latest (cache was browser-side). Poster canvas not re-rendered in harness — regenerate to confirm pill spacing. md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
notes: FOUNDER: the cache-bust is why earlier fixes 'didn't work' — hard-refresh once to pick up the versioned assets. Future CSS changes need a ?v bump (or a Cloudflare purge) to propagate.
